Tacos Worth
Getting Inked For.

Slow-braised birria, carne asada, and Nashville hot chicken, served fast. Find us on campus or bring us to yours.

The Menu

Fresh Ink,
Every Day.

Fan Favorite
Birria Queso Tacos
Shredded beef birria, corn tortilla, Chihuahua cheese, cilantro, onion & consommé for dipping
Carne Asada Tacos
Carne asada steak, guacamole, refried beans, cilantro, diced onions, Chihuahua cheese
Nashville Hot
Hot Chicken Tacos
Nashville hot chicken, Comeback salsa, diced pickles, coleslaw & Chihuahua cheese
Signature
Tattoo Blue Tacos
Ground beef in a crispy blue shell, Chihuahua cheese, shredded lettuce, choice of salsa
Family Nachos
Tortilla chips, black beans, white queso, jalapeños, sour cream, guacamole & choice of meat
Inked Original
Birria Sliders
Shredded birria beef on toasted slider buns with consommé dipping sauce

For Campus & Venue Operators

Built for
Non-Traditional Venues.

LIVE INSTALLATION: CO-BRANDED SERVICE LINE
BRANDED DINING ENVIRONMENT

Inked Tacos runs in university dining halls, travel plazas, airports, and ghost kitchens. The menu is short. The equipment footprint is small. Your existing staff can learn it in days.

Inked Tacos is one of five Experiential Brands concepts. Operators can run it next to FlameTown Burgers, The Original Hot Chicken, Duck Duck Dragon, or Pinsa Roman Pizza on the same service line, all under one license.

1
License covers all five EB brands
5
Brands run from one kitchen
19M+
College students in the US
6
University campuses operating today
  • Works in your existing kitchen. No dedicated hood, no build-out
  • Prep cards, build cards, and photo-referenced procedure guides for training
  • License terms written for institutional foodservice
  • Start with one pilot location before committing across your portfolio
Ask About Licensing
The Platform
Five brands. One license.
Inked Tacos FlameTown Burgers The Original Hot Chicken Duck Duck Dragon Pinsa Roman Pizza

Inked Tacos is part of Experiential Brands, a licensing platform for non-traditional venues. One agreement gives operators access to all five concepts.
